summary: Stunning Stephen Stills guest jams and powerful Black Queen
blurb: The show features a historic Stephen Stills appearance on Black Queen, with the band delivering tight jams throughout. Standout moments include Black Peter, Morning Dew and an expansive Black Queen jam that showcases early Live/Dead material.
review: On December 10, 1969 at Thelma Theater the Dead rolled out a rare guest appearance by Stephen Stills, who sits in on guitar for a snarling, jug‑band‑style duel with Jerry during the towering Black Queen. The first half of the set wanders through familiar material—China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider, Black Peter and a handful of country‑rock cuts—but it’s the second half that truly shines: Stills adds sweet textures on Good Morning Little School Girl and an epic, emotionally charged Morning Dew, while the band locks into a seamless Black Queen jam that feels both spacious and tightly knit. The matrix recording captures this chemistry with crystal‑clear balance, making the improvisations on Cryptical Envelopment, The Other One and Cosmic Charlie (though not listed in the official setlist) stand out as some of the most cohesive performances of the era. For fans of late‑’60s Dead, this show is a historic snapshot of a band testing new Live/Dead concepts with an unforgettable guest star.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All three recordings are marked as positive and consistently praise the same highlights—Stephen Stills' guest work, the Black Queen jam, and the epic Morning Dew. Multiple reviewers use enthusiastic language such as "excellent", "stellar" and "wild edge", indicating strong positive sentiment across sources.

best_recording.reason: Soundboard capture with clear vintage sound, full set present and praised for its crisp mix and minimal drop‑outs.

key_highlights: Stephen Stills joins on guitar, adding a wild edge to Black Queen and Schoolgirl.
The Black Queen jam stands out as an expansive, cohesive improvisation.
Morning Dew delivers an epic, emotionally charged performance with guest contributions.
Matrix recording offers crystal‑clear sound that highlights the band’s tight interplay.

band_performance.Jerry: Shows snarling, stinging guitar work especially during the jug‑band style jam with Stephen Stills on Black Queen.
